# MCP Dart SDK

`mcp_dart` is a community Dart and Flutter SDK for Model Context Protocol
(MCP) servers, clients, and hosts.

- SDK preview: `2.3.0-dev.2`
- CLI preview: `0.2.0-dev.2`
- Published SDK preview minimum: Dart 3.5
- Unreleased SDK source minimum: Dart 3.4
- CLI preview minimum: Dart 3.7
- Unreleased CLI source minimum: Dart 3.12
- Repository: https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart
- Package: https://pub.dev/packages/mcp_dart
- License: MIT

## Release status

The 2.3.0 preview implements the complete core client/server wire surface of
the locked MCP `2026-07-28` release candidate, retains the MCP `2025-11-25`
feature set, and negotiates supported earlier initialization versions. Core
means the normative wire requirements assigned to client and server roles by
the pinned preview specification; it excludes optional extensions, host UI
behavior, an authorization-server implementation, JSON Schema external
reference resolution, and custom JSON Schema vocabularies. The official
alpha.9 MCP 2026-07-28 client suite passes against current source, including
all 25 authorization scenarios. Its server suite has three exact expected
diagnostics because the published referee predates spec PR #3002; merged
conformance PR #403 semantics pass locally. This is prerelease evidence, not a
claim about the not-yet-published final specification.

Use the latest stable SDK for production:

```bash
dart pub add mcp_dart
```

Select the preview explicitly:

```yaml
dependencies:
  mcp_dart: ^2.3.0-dev.2
```

## Primary import

```dart
import 'package:mcp_dart/mcp_dart.dart';
```

## Protocol profiles

- `McpProtocol.stable` (default dual-era profile): prefer MCP 2026-07-28
  stateless discovery, then fall back to legacy initialization when the peer is
  legacy-only. On body-only transports such as stdio, the discovery probe is
  bounded to 5 seconds; HTTP retains its normal request timeout.
- `McpProtocol.legacy` (initialization-era profile): negotiate MCP 2025-11-25
  or an earlier supported initialization-era version.
- `McpProtocol.require2026`: require the MCP 2026-07-28 stateless protocol.

```dart
final client = McpClient(
  const Implementation(name: 'client', version: '1.0.0'),
  options: const McpClientOptions(protocol: McpProtocol.legacy),
);
```

2026-only APIs, examples, and fallback rules:
https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/blob/v2.3.0-dev.2/doc/mcp-2026-07-28.md

Upgrade requirements and application-visible changes from 2.2 to 2.3:
https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/blob/main/doc/migration-2.2-to-2.3.md

Strict MCP 2026-07-28 runnable example:
https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/tree/v2.3.0-dev.2/example/mcp_2026_07_28

## Main capabilities

- Tools and JSON Schema input/output.
- Resources, URI templates, and subscriptions.
- Prompts and completion.
- Sampling, roots, elicitation, progress, logging, and cancellation. MCP 2026-07-28
  logging is implemented for compatibility but deprecated upstream; new
  examples use progress and subscriptions instead.
- A Tasks extension implementation, generic extension negotiation, and typed
  MCP Apps metadata helpers. Extensions are separate from core coverage.
- Text, image, audio, embedded resource, and resource-link content.

Always check negotiated capabilities before an optional operation. Advertising
a capability and implementing its runtime behavior are separate obligations.

## Server skeleton

```dart
final server = McpServer(
  const Implementation(name: 'example-server', version: '1.0.0'),
  options: const McpServerOptions(
    capabilities: ServerCapabilities(
      tools: ServerCapabilitiesTools(),
      resources: ServerCapabilitiesResources(),
      prompts: ServerCapabilitiesPrompts(),
    ),
  ),
);

server.registerTool(
  'add',
  description: 'Add two numbers',
  inputSchema: JsonSchema.object(
    properties: {
      'a': JsonSchema.number(),
      'b': JsonSchema.number(),
    },
    required: ['a', 'b'],
  ),
  callback: (arguments, extra) async => CallToolResult(
    content: [
      TextContent(
        text: '${(arguments['a'] as num) + (arguments['b'] as num)}',
      ),
    ],
  ),
);

await server.connect(StdioServerTransport());
```

Detailed server APIs:
https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/blob/v2.3.0-dev.2/doc/server-guide.md

## Client skeleton

```dart
final client = McpClient(
  const Implementation(name: 'example-client', version: '1.0.0'),
);

await client.connect(
  StdioClientTransport(
    const StdioServerParameters(
      command: 'dart',
      args: ['run', 'bin/server.dart'],
    ),
  ),
);

try {
  final tools = await client.listTools();
  final result = await client.callTool(
    const CallToolRequest(
      name: 'add',
      arguments: {'a': 2, 'b': 3},
    ),
  );
} finally {
  await client.close();
}
```

Detailed client APIs:
https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/blob/v2.3.0-dev.2/doc/client-guide.md

## Transports

| Target | Recommended transport |
| --- | --- |
| Dart VM or desktop local helper | Stdio |
| Remote service | Streamable HTTP |
| Browser or Flutter Web | Streamable HTTP client |
| Flutter mobile | Remote Streamable HTTP; app-managed helper for local IPC |
| In-process tests | IO stream or custom transport |

Stdio servers must reserve stdout for MCP JSON-RPC frames and write logs to
stderr. Browser apps cannot spawn stdio processes.

For Streamable HTTP, keep DNS rebinding protection enabled and configure exact
Host and Origin allowlists. Use TLS for remote endpoints.

Transport details:
https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/blob/v2.3.0-dev.2/doc/transports.md

## Authentication and security

The SDK exposes OAuth client providers, protected-resource/authorization-server
discovery, PKCE S256 helpers, bearer challenges, and server authentication
callbacks. These are protocol and transport building blocks, not an identity
platform.

The checked-in OAuth examples persist tokens in plaintext JSON files for local
learning. Production applications must replace that storage with a system
keychain, platform secure storage, or an encrypted credential service.

OAuth examples:
https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/tree/v2.3.0-dev.2/example/authentication

## Errors

- Return `CallToolResult(isError: true, ...)` for expected tool-domain errors.
- Throw `McpError` with the correct `ErrorCode` for MCP/JSON-RPC failures.
- Use `StateError` for invalid object state and `ArgumentError` for invalid
  caller input.
- Preserve JSON-RPC request IDs, progress tokens, method names, metadata, and
  error codes in their wire-compatible shape.

## CLI

Install the preview after its matching SDK is public:

```bash
dart pub global activate mcp_dart_cli 0.2.0-dev.2
```

Common commands:

```bash
mcp_dart create my_server
# Terminal 1
mcp_dart serve --transport http --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000
# Terminal 2
mcp_dart inspect --url http://localhost:3000/mcp
mcp_dart inspect-server --json -- node server.js
mcp_dart inspect-client --report /tmp/client-report.json
mcp_dart trace --report /tmp/trace.json -- node server.js
mcp_dart list-tools -- python server.py
mcp_dart call-tool echo --json-args '{"message":"hello"}' -- python server.py
mcp_dart conformance --suite all
```

The CLI inspector reports only behavior it exercises. It is not a formal
certification suite for arbitrary targets. Prerelease automatic updates are
disabled so the stable channel cannot replace a preview installation.

CLI docs:
https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/tree/mcp_dart_cli-v0.2.0-dev.2/packages/mcp_dart_cli

## Verification evidence

- Root Dart analyzer and unit/integration tests.
- Official MCP 2025-11-25 server/client conformance paths.
- MCP 2026-07-28 alpha.9 client coverage, including all 25 authorization
  scenarios, plus exactly three stale expected server diagnostics.
- Bidirectional published TypeScript SDK beta interop, plus Python SDK beta
  server interop with an exact expected-gap check for its pre-#3002 client.
- Real Chrome cross-origin Streamable HTTP smoke coverage.
- Checked-in spec example audit pinned to an upstream commit.

Do not infer final MCP 2026-07-28 coverage from those gates. On the official
spec tag, diff schemas, examples, conformance, and normative prose; then rerun
the full day-0 release gate.

Evidence and runbooks:

- https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/blob/v2.3.0-dev.2/doc/interoperability.md
- https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/blob/v2.3.0-dev.2/doc/spec-coverage-2025-11-25.md
- https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/blob/v2.3.0-dev.2/doc/spec-coverage-2026-07-28.md
- https://github.com/leehack/mcp_dart/blob/v2.3.0-dev.2/doc/mcp-2026-07-28-release-runbook.md

- Other migrations: `doc/migration-cookbooks.md`
- Examples: `doc/examples.md` and `example/`
